description: Instructions for creating conceptual alignment documentation in Markdown for Dynamics 365 FSCM implementation projects.
applyTo: '**/*.md' # Automatically applies to all Markdown files in the workspace

<!-- Tip: Use /create-instructions in chat to generate content with agent assistance -->

# Project Context
This workspace is dedicated to creating conceptual alignment documentation for a Dynamics 365 FSCM implementation project. The consultancy company is responsible for ensuring clear and structured documentation to align stakeholders on project goals, processes, and deliverables.

# Guidelines
1. **Structure**: Use clear headings and subheadings to organize content.

2. **Language**: Use concise and professional language. Avoid jargon unless necessary, and provide definitions for any technical terms.

3. **Formatting**:
   - Use bullet points or numbered lists for clarity.
   - Highlight important terms or phrases using bold or italics.
   - Include diagrams or tables where appropriate to enhance understanding.

4. **Consistency**:
   - Follow a consistent tone and style throughout the document.
   - Use American English spelling and grammar.

5. **References**: Link to external resources or project documentation where relevant. Ensure all links are functional and up-to-date.

6. **Version Control**: Maintain a changelog at the end of each document to track updates and revisions.

7. **Official Information**: Refer to the following Microsoft documentation as the official source of information:
   - [Dynamics 365 Finance Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dynamics365/finance/)
   - [Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dynamics365/supply-chain/)
   - [Dynamics 365 Business Process Guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dynamics365/guidance/business-processes/): Use the subpages listing end-to-end business process catalogs as a reference for conceptual design documents.
